summary: Merged a large decomp-matching update for the event match menu in src/melee/mn/mnevent.c, adding data constants and implementations for event row rendering, selected-event result text, menu object setup, cleanup, and controller navigation. decomp.dev reported +796 matched bytes: mnEvent_8024D5B0 and fn_8024E1B4 became 100% matches, with partial improvements for fn_8024D864, mnEvent_8024D15C, and mnEvent_8024E524. The slice contains no human review discussion.

key_files.role: Primary PR target; +436/-12 lines. Added static data and several event-menu functions, plus a cleanup rewrite that reached 100% match.
Minor matching/naming change; renamed inline_fn to GET_EVENTDATA and updated its one callsite. The diff shows no behavior change.

what_changed: Added baselib/debug.h to mnevent.c so the new HSD_ASSERTREPORT call in mnEvent_8024E524 can compile and match.

Added mnevent static data: Vec3 offsets mnEvent_803EF764 and mnEvent_803EF770, Shift-JIS/format byte strings such as mnEvent_803EF77C, mnEvent_803EF794, and mnEvent_803EF7A0, color words mnEvent_804D5028 and mnEvent_804D502C, asset pointer globals mnEvent_804A08F8 and mnEvent_804A0908, and small format/placeholder strings mnEvent_804D5040 and mnEvent_804D5044.

Implemented mnEvent_8024D15C as an event-list row renderer: it finds JObj markers 0xA and 0xC, computes row spacing, removes old row GObjs/text/icons, checks unlock state with gmMainLib_8015CEFC, creates an icon GObj from mnEvent_804A0908 when unlocked, and creates HSD_Text entries for event number and icon glyph.

Implemented mnEvent_8024D5B0 for the selected event's name_text/result display. It recreates the HSD_Text, sets position/color/alignment/font size, uses gmMainLib_8015CF5C and gm_801BEB8C/gmMainLib_8015CEFC to decide time-vs-number-vs-locked formatting, and emits text through HSD_SisLib_803A6B98.

Reworked fn_8024E1B4 cleanup to use a new inline GET_EVENTDATA(gobj), cache gobj->hsd_obj as tree, and iterate through gobjs/texts/icons using a 4-byte pointer-step alias before deleting the parent GObj.

Implemented mnEvent_8024E524 to create and initialize the event menu GObj: load joint/animation assets from mnEvent_804A08F8, allocate MnEventData with HSD_MemAlloc and HSD_ASSERTREPORT, initialize user data, position the page/cursor JObj 0xB from JObj 0xA/0xC spacing, install fn_8024E34C as a process, populate nine visible rows, and refresh selected event panels/animations.

Implemented fn_8024D864 as the event menu input handler: handles cooldown, Back, Confirm, X/Y page jumps, Up/Down cursor movement and scrolling, audio feedback, event selection via gm_801BEB74/gm_801677E8/mn_80229860, row repopulation, and repeated selected-event UI refreshes.

Renamed vi1201v2.c inline helper inline_fn to GET_EVENTDATA and updated the call in un_803207C4; the helper still returns GObj_Create(0xE, 0xF, 0), so the diff only supports treating this as a matching/naming artifact.
smart_moves: Used JObj locator nodes 0xA, 0xC, 0xB, and 0x9 to derive list spacing, cursor position, and selected-event animation state from the menu asset instead of hardcoding every position.
Preserved exact packed color writes with *(s32*) &text->text_color for HSD_Text, matching the original style of writing RGBA-like color words.

Declared exact-size byte arrays for Shift-JIS strings, placeholders, format strings, and adjacent debug/asset-name data, improving data-section matching rather than approximating with normal C string literals only.
Used a small inline GET_EVENTDATA wrapper in fn_8024E1B4; the bot report shows that function improved from 50.90% to 100%, making this an example of inline-helper-driven codegen matching.

Matched awkward pointer arithmetic over MnEventData with row = (MnEventData*) ((u8*) data + idx * 4) and iter = (MnEventData*) ((u8*) iter + 4), even though ordinary data->gobjs[idx] style would be cleaner.

Kept the refresh call order consistent after every selection/page movement: rebuild rows when needed, compute event through gm_801BEBA8, update mnEvent_8024D0CC/mnEvent_8024D7E0/mnEvent_8024D5B0, animate JObj 9, then call mnEvent_8024D014.
decomp_lessons: For menu decomp, asset-space JObj child IDs can be the key to reconstructing list spacing and cursor motion; preserve the lb_80011E24 lookup IDs while semantics are still uncertain.
When the original compiler indexes parallel pointer arrays by byte offset, matching may require struct-pointer alias tricks rather than idiomatic array indexing.
Do not over-rename gm_801BEBxx/gmMainLib calls based only on local behavior; this PR kept most unknown gm functions address-named while exposing their use in event unlock/result logic.
HSD_ASSERTREPORT matching needs the debug header, exact line value, and exact message text; here mnEvent_8024E524 adds HSD_ASSERTREPORT(0x39B, user_data, "Can't get user_data.\n").
Stack buffer sizes can matter for text formatting; mnEvent_8024D5B0 uses three char[4] buffers with mn_8022EA78 for two-digit time pieces.
Small helper names can be codegen artifacts. The vi1201v2 GET_EVENTDATA helper returns GObj_Create, so future readers should not infer semantics from that name without more evidence.
The decomp.dev bot report is strong evidence for matching progress, but absence of human PR body/review means intent and naming rationale remain weakly evidenced.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names were retained for newly decompiled mnevent functions: mnEvent_8024D15C, mnEvent_8024D5B0, mnEvent_8024E524, and fn_8024D864.
Callback/proc-style functions remained fn_8024xxxx where semantics were not confidently named, such as fn_8024D864 and fn_8024E1B4.
Static data kept address-derived names like mnEvent_803EF764, mnEvent_803EF77C, mnEvent_804D5028, mnEvent_804A08F8, and mnEvent_804A0908.
Local JObj names such as jobj_0A, jobj_0C, jobj_0B, and jobj_09 mirror lb_80011E24 child IDs and are useful temporary names before asset-node semantics are known.
GET_EVENTDATA was introduced as an inline helper in mnevent.c for gobj->user_data access; the same name appearing in vi1201v2.c is not semantically reliable based on this diff.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inline helper tactic: inline MnEventData* GET_EVENTDATA(HSD_GObj* gobj) { return gobj->user_data; } was used in fn_8024E1B4, which the bot reported as a new 100% match.
Codegen-sensitive caching: fn_8024E1B4 stores gobj->hsd_obj in a local tree before calling mn_8022EC18 instead of repeatedly using gobj->hsd_obj.
Pointer-step cleanup loop: fn_8024E1B4 uses both tmp->gobjs[i]/texts[i]/icons[i] and iter->gobjs[0]/texts[0]/icons[0], then advances iter by 4 bytes per iteration.
Preserved casts and masks in gm calls, including u8 event truncation and expressions like event & 0xFFFFFFFFu / 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFu, likely for exact register/compare behavior.
Used exact floating constants for text and layout, including 0.035f, 0.03f, 17.0f, 364.68332f, 38.38772f, 3.8f, 4.25f, and 6.9f.

Recovered data sections with exact arrays and symbol placement; decomp.dev reported .data improvement from 26.29% to 91.29%, .bss from 0.00% to 66.67%, .sdata2 from 61.67% to 70.00%, and .sbss from 0.00% to 50.00%.
review_feedback: No human PR body text was provided.
No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code 66.40% (+0.02%, +796 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported 2 new matches: mnEvent_8024D5B0 +560 bytes from 0.00% to 100.00%, and fn_8024E1B4 +116 bytes from 50.90% to 100.00%.
decomp.dev bot reported 7 unmatched-item improvements: fn_8024D864 to 80.67%, mnEvent_8024D15C to 81.16%, mnEvent_8024E524 to 83.09%, plus .data/.bss/.sdata2/.sbss improvements.
decomp.dev bot reported one small unmatched-item regression: main/melee/mn/mnevent .sdata -1 byte, 57.78% to 55.56%.
